findings.evidence_quote: El Adelanto de Reembolso de TurboTax es un préstamo sobre la base de tu reembolso anticipado y no es el reembolso en sí. La tasa de porcentaje anual es del 0 % y se cobran $0 de comisiones por préstamo.
findings.location: TurboTax Online offers/pricing footnotes - Refund Advance loan details
findings.reasoning: Trigger: a rate of finance charge is stated for the Refund Advance loan product. Requirement: the rate is expressed as 'tasa de porcentaje anual' (Annual Percentage Rate/APR) which is the Spanish equivalent of APR - '0%'. This satisfies R-02's requirement that any stated finance charge rate be expressed as an APR. Axis A compliant. No specific library entry governs APR phrasing distinctly from the general disclosure text itself, so axis B is na. Counter-reading: one might argue 'tasa de porcentaje anual' is not literally the acronym 'APR' and thus doesn't count as 'stated as an APR', but this is simply the Spanish-language equivalent term for Annual Percentage Rate, which is standard and functionally identical - rejected as overly literal.

findings.evidence_quote: La tasa de porcentaje anual variable oscila entre el 15 % y el 33 %. Por ejemplo, un préstamo de $2,000 a 6 meses con una tasa de porcentaje anual del 19 % tiene un cargo financiero de $112.28 y 6 pagos a plazos mensuales de $352.05 cada uno.
findings.location: TurboTax Online offers/pricing footnotes - File Now, Pay Later loan details
findings.reasoning: Trigger: a rate of finance charge is stated for the File Now, Pay Later loan product, including an explicit 'cargo financiero' (finance charge) dollar amount tied to an APR example. Requirement: the rate is expressed as 'tasa de porcentaje anual' (APR), ranging 15%-33%, with a worked example showing the APR (19%) alongside the finance charge amount - this is the Reg Z-style APR + finance-charge disclosure pattern. Axis A compliant since the rate is properly stated as an APR. Axis B na as no distinct approved library wording is being checked here separately from the general finance disclosure. Counter-reading: could argue the range (15%-33%) alone without a single fixed rate might not clearly qualify as 'the finance charge stated as an APR', but the required Reg Z disclosure format explicitly ties finance charge to APR and this is compliant.

findings.evidence_quote: La tasa de porcentaje anual es del 0 % y se cobran $0 de comisiones por préstamo.
findings.location: File Now, Pay Later loan details section
findings.reasoning: Rule R-02 triggers when any rate of finance charge is stated. In the File Now, Pay Later disclosure, the page explicitly states "La tasa de porcentaje anual es del 0 %", which is a rate of finance charge and is clearly expressed as an APR ("tasa de porcentaje anual" is the standard Spanish term for APR). Therefore the requirement that any finance charge rate be stated as an APR is satisfied. No conflicting finance-charge rate phrasing appears elsewhere on the page. There is no separate library-approved wording requirement for APR language under this rule, so axis B is not applicable. The only possible counterargument is that another example sentence describes a finance charge in dollars ($112.28) and monthly payments, but that example is paired with the APR statement and does not introduce any non-APR rate; thus it does not violate the rule. The page is compliant where triggered.

findings.evidence_quote: La tasa de porcentaje anual es del 0 % y se cobran $0 de comisiones por préstamo.
findings.location: TurboTax Online/Dispositivos móviles section – Adelanto de Reembolso de TurboTax loan details
findings.reasoning: The page states a rate of finance charge for the Refund Advance and expresses it as an APR (“tasa de porcentaje anual” = APR). Therefore the rule triggers and the requirement is met. No approved-library phrasing governs this rule, so Axis B is not applicable. Strongest counter-reading: one might argue that using the Spanish term instead of the APR acronym could be noncompliant; however, “tasa de porcentaje anual” is the standard Spanish term for APR and fulfills the requirement to state the rate as APR.

findings.evidence_quote: La tasa de porcentaje anual variable oscila entre el 15 % y el 33 %.
findings.location: TurboTax Online/Dispositivos móviles section – File Now, Pay Later loan details
findings.reasoning: A finance charge rate for the File Now, Pay Later loan is stated and clearly labeled as APR (“tasa de porcentaje anual”), satisfying the rule. No library-governed phrasing applies, so Axis B is not applicable. Strongest counter-reading: one could claim only ranges were given; however, the rule requires that if a finance charge rate is stated, it be expressed as APR, which it is.
